freshness_caveat: Storage data 2 days old (as_of 2026-06-10); TTF and EUA 1 day old (as_of 2026-06-11). DE Power, GB Power, Clean Spark, Clean Dark current (as_of 2026-06-12).
risk_flags: EU storage at 43.36% (19th pctile, 14.3 pp below 5-yr seasonal) — summer refill pace critical if Hormuz closure sustains.
Clean Spark at 16.77 EUR/MWh (74th pctile) after 15.2σ move — suggests rapid merit-order rotation into gas-fired generation on oil-product signals.
Hormuz closure restricts 20%+ global crude exports; sustained disruption forces LNG redirect and raises European gas burn-through risk.
Trump-Iran escalation could tighten crude further, reinforcing fuel-switch economics favoring thermal over storage discharge.

base.summary: Hormuz closure persists; storage refill steady; DE power tracks current thermal premium.

downside.summary: Hormuz reopens; crude rally reverses; storage refill accelerates; fuel-switch margin compresses.

upside.summary: Hormuz remains blocked; Trump-Iran escalation tightens crude; LNG redirects to Asia; European thermal dispatch extends.

themes: Hormuz closure + Iran escalation lifting crude/power spreads
Storage deficit pressures summer margin; fuel-switching upside to thermal
Clean Spark outsized volatility signals merit-order instability
top_drivers: Hormuz closure (20%+ crude exports disrupted) + Iran-Trump escalation driving Brent rally and oil-to-gas switching in European power merit order.
Storage deficit (43.36%, 14.3 pp below seasonal) compressing summer margin and raising thermal dispatch value through H2.
Clean Spark 15.2σ spike reflects rapid repricing of coal-to-gas substitution under geopolitical crude premium.
top_takeaway: Clean Spark explodes 15.2σ on Hormuz-driven oil rally; EU storage 14.3 pp below seasonal raises H2 refill urgency amid geopolitical supply risk.

watchlist.why: Sustained disruption redirects LNG supply chains and raises global oil prices; direct transmission into European gas burn-through and TTF via fuel-switching.
At 43.36% (14.3 pp below seasonal), refill pace through summer will determine H2 power curve shape and thermal dispatch risk.
High Brent on Hormuz closure incentivizes gas-to-oil switching in power generation; widening spreads extend thermal call into Q3.
Further escalation could tighten crude supply, sustain elevated Brent, and reinforce power spread upside.
